stack: Laravel 12 + Inertia.js v2 + React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS v4

strengths: Dynamic page titles via Inertia <Head> on all pages
Canonical URLs on every public page (blade default + per-page override)
Auth-aware meta robots: noindex/nofollow for authenticated users, index/follow for public
Open Graph tags (og:type, og:site_name, og:url, og:image) on all public pages
Twitter Card summary_large_image on all content pages
Charset, viewport, and lang attribute properly configured
google-extended meta tag for AI crawler control

findings.title: Single default OG image for all pages
findings.description: All pages share og-default.png. No per-page or per-feature custom OG images exist. Social sharing previews will look identical across all pages, reducing click-through differentiation.
findings.recommendation: Generate unique OG images for key landing pages (pricing, features, blog posts, comparison pages) using a dynamic OG image service or static assets per page.

findings.title: WelcomeController passes no explicit SEO props
findings.description: The homepage relies entirely on app.blade.php defaults for meta description and OG tags. No page-specific meta description is set for the most important page.
findings.recommendation: Pass explicit metaDescription, ogTitle, and ogDescription props from WelcomeController to allow page-level SEO customization.

findings.title: No meta description in blade template defaults
findings.description: app.blade.php has no fallback <meta name='description'> tag. Pages without explicit meta descriptions via Inertia <Head> will have no description tag at all.
findings.recommendation: Add a default <meta name='description' content='...' inertia> tag in app.blade.php that Inertia can override per-page.

findings.title: Missing og:description in blade defaults
findings.description: Open Graph description is not set in the base template. Pages that don't explicitly set og:description via Inertia Head will have no OG description for social sharing.
findings.recommendation: Add <meta property='og:description' content='{{ config("app.description") }}' /> as a default.

findings.title: No twitter:site or twitter:creator meta tags
findings.description: Twitter Card tags lack @handle attribution. Tweets sharing links won't attribute the site or content author.
findings.recommendation: Add <meta name='twitter:site' content='@yourhandle' /> in the base template.

strengths: All content pages use clean, hyphenated, lowercase slugs
Blog slug validation via regex constraint [a-z0-9-]+
Trailing slash normalization via 301 redirect in .htaccess
Logical hierarchical URL structure: /features/*, /guides/*, /compare/*, /blog/*
No query parameters in content URLs (clean paths only)
Route names defined for all public routes enabling stable URL generation

findings.title: No trailing slash normalization in nginx config
findings.description: deploy/nginx-static-cache.conf handles asset caching but lacks trailing slash rewrite. Apache .htaccess handles it, but nginx deployments may serve duplicate content.
findings.recommendation: Add 'rewrite ^(.*)/$ $1 permanent;' to the nginx deployment config template.

findings.title: Blog slug not normalized on server side
findings.description: Route constraint [a-z0-9-]+ rejects uppercase slugs, but BlogController.loadPost() does not lowercase-normalize slug input. If markdown frontmatter contains uppercase, the route will 404 silently.
findings.recommendation: Add strtolower() normalization in BlogController::loadPost() as a defensive fallback.

findings.title: No explicit 301 redirects for legacy or renamed URLs
findings.description: No redirect mapping exists for URL changes. If page slugs change in the future, old URLs will 404 without redirects.
findings.recommendation: Implement a redirect map (config file or database table) for future URL migrations. Not urgent for a new project.

strengths: Dynamic robots.txt with environment-aware rules (blocks all crawling in non-production)
Comprehensive XML sitemap with 40+ URLs, priorities (0.3-1.0), changefreq, and lastmod
Sitemap cached with 24-hour TTL for performance
Three JSON-LD schemas in blade: SoftwareApplication, Organization, FAQPage
Additional schemas in React: BlogPosting, BreadcrumbList, FaqJsonLd per-page
llms.txt following llmstxt.org spec for AI crawler compliance
CSP nonce integration for inline structured data scripts
SSR enabled via Inertia createServer for crawler-friendly rendering

findings.title: No schema validation or testing infrastructure
findings.description: All structured data is manually implemented without automated validation. Schema errors could go undetected and cause rich result losses.
findings.recommendation: Add structured data validation to CI pipeline using schema.org validator or Google Rich Results Test API.

findings.title: Sitemap lastmod dates are hardcoded for static pages
findings.description: Static pages in buildSitemap() have hardcoded lastmod dates. If page content changes, sitemap won't reflect actual modification time, potentially reducing crawl priority.
findings.recommendation: Use file mtime of the React page component (as done for blog posts) or git last-commit date for more accurate lastmod values.

findings.title: No sitemap index for scalability
findings.description: Single flat sitemap.xml. Currently fine with ~40 URLs, but blog growth could exceed 50,000 URL limit without a sitemap index.
findings.recommendation: Plan for sitemap index structure when blog/content exceeds ~100 URLs.

findings.title: No AggregateRating or Review schema
findings.description: Product/SoftwareApplication schema lacks rating/review data. Missing opportunity for star ratings in SERPs.
findings.recommendation: Add AggregateRating to SoftwareApplication schema once user reviews/ratings exist.

findings.title: Unused sitemap blade template
findings.description: resources/views/seo/sitemap.blade.php exists but SeoController.buildSitemap() generates XML directly. Dead template file.
findings.recommendation: Remove unused template or refactor buildSitemap() to use it for consistency.

findings.title: No structured data for comparison/guide pages
findings.description: Compare and Guide pages lack specific schema types (e.g., Article, HowTo). Only BlogPosting is used for blog content.
findings.recommendation: Add Article or TechArticle schema to guide pages and comparison pages for rich SERP treatment.

strengths: SSR enabled via Inertia createServer with ReactDOMServer.renderToString
Strategic vendor chunk splitting (8 categories) reducing main bundle size by ~598KB
Lazy-loaded UI components (Toaster, FlashToasts, CookieConsent) via React.lazy/Suspense
Font loading optimized: preconnect to CDN + display=swap
Tailwind CSS v4 JIT compilation with automatic purging
Production build drops console/debugger statements
Nginx static asset caching: 1 year immutable for hashed build assets
AddLinkHeadersForPreloadedAssets middleware for HTTP/2 push
Reduced-motion CSS preferences respected

findings.title: No responsive image implementation
findings.description: Zero usage of srcset, sizes attributes, or <picture> element across the entire codebase. All images served at fixed resolution regardless of viewport, wasting bandwidth on mobile and degrading LCP.
findings.recommendation: Implement responsive images with srcset/sizes for marketing page images. Use WebP/AVIF with PNG fallback via <picture> element.

findings.title: Inconsistent image lazy loading
findings.description: Only FeatureScreenshot.tsx uses loading='lazy'. All other image locations lack lazy loading, causing unnecessary downloads on initial page load.
findings.recommendation: Add loading='lazy' to all below-the-fold images. Create a shared Image component enforcing lazy loading, width, and height attributes.

findings.title: No explicit HTTP Cache-Control headers for HTML pages
findings.description: SecurityHeaders middleware sets security headers but no Cache-Control for public marketing pages. Browsers and CDNs cannot cache HTML responses without explicit directives.
findings.recommendation: Add Cache-Control: public, max-age=300, s-maxage=3600 for public marketing pages. Keep no-cache for authenticated pages.

findings.title: No PWA manifest or service worker
findings.description: No manifest.webmanifest, no service worker, no offline fallback. Missing PWA signals that contribute to Lighthouse score and engagement metrics.
findings.recommendation: Add a basic web app manifest for installability signals. Service worker for offline fallback is optional but beneficial.

findings.title: No preconnect to analytics or third-party domains
findings.description: Only fonts.bunny.net has preconnect. Google Analytics (www.googletagmanager.com) loads without preconnect hint, adding to connection latency.
findings.recommendation: Add <link rel='preconnect' href='https://www.googletagmanager.com'> when analytics is enabled.

findings.title: No Inertia prefetch configuration
findings.description: Inertia v2 supports prefetching on hover/visibility but no prefetch configuration is set. Users experience full page load latency on every navigation.
findings.recommendation: Configure Inertia prefetch for high-priority navigation links to improve perceived performance.

strengths: Excellent semantic HTML: consistent use of <main>, <section>, <nav>, <article>, <header>, <footer>
Skip-to-content links on all major layouts (Welcome.tsx, DashboardLayout.tsx)
Comprehensive ARIA implementation: aria-label on interactive elements, aria-expanded on menus, aria-current on breadcrumbs
HTML lang attribute dynamically set from Laravel locale
SVG icons use aria-hidden for decorative, aria-label for informational
Focus management on error pages and dialogs
Rich content strategy: 8 guides, 9 comparison pages, blog with BlogPosting schema
Proper error pages (403, 404, 419, 429, 500, 503) with user-friendly messages

findings.title: Missing h1 tags on key marketing pages
findings.description: Pricing.tsx, Buy.tsx, Onboarding.tsx, and Dashboard.tsx lack an <h1> heading. Search engines use h1 as a primary content signal; missing h1 degrades page relevance scoring.
findings.recommendation: Add a single <h1> to each page. For Pricing and Buy (public-facing), this directly impacts SEO. For Dashboard/Onboarding (noindexed), it improves accessibility.

findings.title: Blog content rendered via dangerouslySetInnerHTML
findings.description: Blog/Show.tsx renders markdown-to-HTML content. While DOMPurify is used for sanitization, the rendered content structure depends on markdown authoring quality. No automated heading hierarchy validation.
findings.recommendation: Validate that blog markdown starts with h2 (since page h1 is the title) and doesn't skip heading levels.

findings.title: No image alt text enforcement pattern
findings.description: While SVG icons are properly handled, there is no shared Image component that enforces alt text. As the project grows and adds raster images, alt text may be omitted.
findings.recommendation: Create a shared Image component with required alt prop to prevent future accessibility regressions.

findings.title: Marketing page testimonials lack structured data
findings.description: Welcome.tsx includes testimonials section but no Review or AggregateRating schema. Social proof doesn't generate rich SERP snippets.
findings.recommendation: Add Review schema markup for testimonials on the homepage.

findings.title: FAQ accordion on Welcome.tsx relies on Blade-side schema only
findings.description: FAQ JSON-LD is embedded in app.blade.php conditionally for homepage. If FAQ content in React doesn't match blade schema, they could drift out of sync.
findings.recommendation: Move FAQ schema generation to React (via FaqJsonLd component) co-located with FAQ content to prevent drift.

strengths: No nofollow on any internal links
All pages have canonical URLs preventing duplicate content
Hierarchical index pages (/features, /guides, /compare, /blog) link to child pages
RelatedContent component provides contextual cross-linking on some pages
Welcome.tsx acts as a strong hub linking to most sections
Guides have heavy cross-linking to features and comparison pages

findings.title: Minimal primary navigation coverage
findings.description: PublicNav.tsx only links 4 pages: 3 feature pages + pricing. Blog, Guides, Compare, About, Changelog, Roadmap, and Contact are absent from main navigation. This severely limits crawl discovery and link equity distribution.
findings.recommendation: Expand PublicNav to include at minimum: Features (dropdown with all), Guides, Blog, Pricing, Compare. Consider mega-menu for content-rich sections.

findings.title: Buy page is orphaned from public navigation
findings.description: /buy route exists but is not linked from PublicNav, PublicFooter, or sitemap. Only reachable through obscure Welcome.tsx CTA links. Crawlers may never discover this page.
findings.recommendation: Add /buy to sitemap.xml. Link from pricing page CTA and footer. If page is conversion-critical, add to primary navigation.

findings.title: Compare pages have no cross-linking to each other
findings.description: Individual comparison pages (e.g., /compare/laravel-jetstream) don't link to other comparison pages. Users and crawlers must go back to /compare index to find alternatives.
findings.recommendation: Add 'See also' or 'Other comparisons' section at the bottom of each comparison page linking to 3-4 related comparisons.

findings.title: Feature pages lack cross-linking to related guides
findings.description: Feature detail pages (e.g., /features/billing) don't consistently link to relevant guide pages (e.g., /guides/laravel-stripe-billing-tutorial). Only Billing.tsx links to one guide.
findings.recommendation: Add RelatedContent component to all feature pages linking to corresponding guide, comparison, and blog content.

findings.title: About page has minimal inbound links
findings.description: /about is only linked from Welcome.tsx hero area. Not in PublicNav or PublicFooter. Low link equity signals low importance to crawlers despite being an E-E-A-T signal page.
findings.recommendation: Add About to PublicFooter Company section. Consider adding to PublicNav or as a dropdown item.

findings.title: Footer-only pages receive minimal link equity
findings.description: Changelog, Roadmap, Terms, Privacy are only linked from PublicFooter. Footer links carry less weight than body/nav links. These pages receive minimal crawl priority.
findings.recommendation: Link Changelog and Roadmap from relevant marketing page sections (e.g., Welcome.tsx trust section). Legal pages in footer only is acceptable.

strengths: HTML lang attribute dynamically set from Laravel locale
Application architecture supports future i18n (Laravel localization system available)

findings.title: No hreflang tags implemented
findings.description: Zero hreflang or alternate-language link tags found. If the product targets international markets, search engines cannot serve correct language versions to users in different regions.
findings.recommendation: If targeting international markets: add hreflang tags. If English-only by design, add <link rel='alternate' hreflang='x-default' href='...' /> to signal default language.

findings.title: No locale routing or i18n middleware
findings.description: No URL-based locale detection (/en/, /fr/), no Accept-Language middleware, no locale switching UI. Single-language deployment without explicit language declaration.
findings.recommendation: If single-language is intentional, document as a design decision. Add x-default hreflang. If multi-language planned, implement locale routing early.

findings.title: No Content-Language HTTP header
findings.description: No Content-Language header set in SecurityHeaders middleware. While not a strong ranking signal, it helps browsers and crawlers understand content language.
findings.recommendation: Add Content-Language: en header to SecurityHeaders middleware for explicit language declaration.

top_priorities.title: Expand primary navigation coverage
top_priorities.rationale: Main nav only links 4 pages. Adding Blog, Guides, Compare sections to PublicNav immediately improves crawl discovery for 30+ content pages.

top_priorities.title: Fix orphaned /buy page
top_priorities.rationale: Conversion-critical page is unreachable from navigation and missing from sitemap. Adding links and sitemap entry is a quick win.

top_priorities.title: Add h1 tags to Pricing and Buy pages
top_priorities.rationale: Two public-facing, SEO-relevant pages lack h1 headings. Simple fix with direct ranking impact.

top_priorities.title: Implement responsive images
top_priorities.rationale: Zero srcset/sizes usage. Mobile CWV scores suffer from oversized images. Affects LCP directly.

top_priorities.title: Add cross-linking between comparison pages
top_priorities.rationale: 9 comparison pages with no interconnection. Adding 'see also' sections creates an internal link cluster boosting topical authority.

top_priorities.title: Cross-link features to related guides
top_priorities.rationale: Feature pages and guide pages cover the same topics but don't link to each other. Natural cross-linking opportunity.

top_priorities.title: Add default meta description in blade template
top_priorities.rationale: Pages without explicit Inertia <Head> meta descriptions have no description tag at all. Quick template fix.

top_priorities.title: Standardize image lazy loading
top_priorities.rationale: Only 1 of N image locations uses loading='lazy'. Quick attribute additions reduce initial page weight.

top_priorities.title: Add x-default hreflang declaration
top_priorities.rationale: Even for English-only sites, an x-default hreflang helps search engines understand language targeting intent.

top_priorities.title: Add Cache-Control headers for public pages
top_priorities.rationale: No explicit caching directives for HTML responses. CDNs and browsers cannot cache pages without Cache-Control headers.
